title: stapelia marginata
intro: Stapelia marginata, also known as the carrion flower or starfish flower, is a succulent plant species in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its unique appearance and distinctive odor that resembles rotting flesh. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Stapelia marginata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts are often used to create topical creams, ointments, and salves, as well as teas and infusions for internal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
